tools.importTitle: Import JSON as editable text layers
tools.importSummary: Use this format when OCR, an AI layout tool, or an external translation team sends draft text positions. Imported text layers are an editable starting point, not a final result to trust right away.
tools.step1Detail: Open the library and pick the title, language, and chapter that should receive the text
tools.step2Detail: Drop or choose a JSON file; the system matches pages and creates draft text layers
tools.step3Detail: Check bbox, text, credits, and AI/QC results before exporting
tools.importKeyRuleTitle: Use source-image coordinates and let the system build a draft layer
tools.importKeyRuleBody: Good JSON must say clearly which page the text is on, and bbox must be measured from the source image's pixels. If the system can't match a page, that entry is skipped to avoid placing it on the wrong page.
tools.draftPolicyBody1: bbox from OCR/AI can be off, so Manga Editor imports text as a movable, resizable layer with conservative auto-fit so you can fix position, font, and wording fast.
tools.draftPolicyBody2: Credit layers should use {protectedCode}. A protected credit stays visible but is treated as attribution content that shouldn't be edited by accident.
tools.checksSummaryStrong: Keep text off the wrong page
tools.folderTitle: Path and filename example from Windows
tools.folderBody: The importer only compares the safe filename tail, so paths from a local tool still match pages in the project without trusting the whole directory.
tools.partialTitle: Match page 5 in the JSON to a single uploaded image
tools.partialBody: If the job has fewer images than the JSON file, the page editor asks which page in the JSON should map to the current image. The fields below let a script state this mapping directly.
tools.acceptedKey1: targetPageIndex: the page in the job that should receive the chosen JSON page
tools.acceptedKey2: sourcePageIndex/sourcePageNumber: pick one JSON page from a large file
tools.acceptedKey3: sourceImagePath/sourceImageName: pick one source image from a large JSON
tools.acceptedKey5: imageId, imageName, fileName, filename: match the page/image by exact match
tools.acceptedKey6: image_path, imagePath, path: match safely from the filename tail
tools.acceptedKey7: a top-level pageIndex or image_path can be a fallback for entries/items
tools.supportedField0: entries or items: the list of text regions to import
tools.supportedField5: category or cat: dialogue, sfx, credit, note
tools.supportedField8: protected: true for a credit or note that shouldn't be edited
tools.importCheck0: Use the source image's pixels for bbox, not the preview or browser CSS pixels
tools.importCheck1: Use pageIndex/pageNumber when you know the page order in the chapter
tools.importCheck2: Use imageName/fileName/image_path when importing a long chapter from an image folder
tools.importCheck3: If importing a single image from a whole-chapter JSON, choose the page in the JSON and match it to the current image
tools.importCheck4: If the image identifier doesn't match the open job, that entry is skipped
tools.importCheck5: Always double-check the import, since bbox from external OCR/AI is a draft position

pricing.metaDescription: Pricing for the manga/webtoon translation workspace: Free, Creator, Pro, Studio, and Studio+
pricing.heading: Choose a plan that fits your team size and workload
pricing.lede: Start on Free to try real work, then upgrade as your team and AI ops grow — no hidden fees, cancel anytime.
pricing.byoBody: For the Studio plan: connect your own OpenAI / Anthropic / other provider API key to your workspace. We call your provider directly and the costs bill to your account — we handle the queue, monitoring, fallback, and usage ledger for you.
pricing.faq1q: Do I need a credit card to start free?
pricing.faq1a: No. Free starts a private workspace with 2 GB storage and 100 AI credits per month.
pricing.faq2q: How is the 20% yearly discount calculated?
pricing.faq2a: The shown price is billed once per year (Dodo charges upfront), which works out to roughly 20% off the monthly rate per month.
pricing.faq3q: Can I change or cancel my plan anytime?
pricing.faq3a: Anytime, from Settings → Billing → Manage subscription (opens the Dodo portal). After cancelling, you keep access until the end of the cycle.
pricing.faq4a: Each AI image costs credits by quality: 10 for low, 90 for medium, 360 for high. Monthly plan credits reset each billing cycle; top-up packs never block your work mid-chapter.
pricing.faq5a: Studio already supports large teams. For white-label, a custom domain, more members, SLA/DPA, or volume deals, contact sales@example.com for a quote.
pricing.faq6q: Why is the Free plan so limited?
pricing.faq6a: Free is meant for trying real work, not as free file storage — if you go over the included limits, we notify you and let you upgrade only for what you exceed.
pricing.faq7q: What happens if I use up my AI credits that month?
pricing.faq7a: New AI jobs are queued/temporarily blocked, while existing work stays open and editable as normal. You can buy an extra credit pack as a one-time purchase.
pricing.faq8a: The frontend is served via a CloudFlare-class CDN, work files live in object storage (R2/equivalent), and the database runs on Postgres in EU-west for GDPR compliance from day one.
pricing.faq9a: Yes — receipts and payment history are available anytime in the Dodo Payments portal via the Manage subscription button.
pricing.faq10q: Want a custom deal or a quote?
pricing.faq10a: Contact sales@example.com — the team is happy to prepare a quote and set up SSO / DPA before you start.
pricing.wsModalDesc: You don't have a workspace to subscribe with yet — create a new workspace first, then come back to choose a plan.

pricing.tierStudioTagline: Large studios with a dedicated QC team
